metadata.title: Project Nimbus: CloudMetrics Acquisition
metadata.author: Apex Capital Partners | Deal Team
metadata.description: Investment Committee memorandum for the proposed acquisition of CloudMetrics Inc., a $200M ARR vertical SaaS platform serving mid-market healthcare providers

elements.content: Investment Committee Memorandum | Apex Capital Partners | Fund VII | Confidential

slides.speaker_notes: Presenting Project Nimbus for IC approval. CloudMetrics is a $200M ARR vertical SaaS platform serving 1,200 mid-market healthcare providers. We are seeking approval for a $1.8B acquisition at 9.0x NTM revenue.

rows: Founder liquidity + growth capital for expansion into payer vertical

slides.speaker_notes: Key highlights: 32% growth, 128% NRR, 82% gross margins. This is a Rule of 57 business. The proprietary sourcing gives us a 4-week exclusivity window before the company runs a broader process.

items: MARKET: Healthcare IT TAM expanding from $48B (2025) to $92B (2030) driven by regulatory mandates (TEFCA, CMS interoperability rules) and value-based care transition
MOAT: CloudMetrics has 78% market share in mid-market RCM analytics -- switching costs are 18+ months of implementation, creating near-zero churn (1.2% gross revenue churn)
EXPANSION: Proven land-and-expand motion: average contract value grew from $82K to $165K over 3 years via module upsells (clinical analytics, population health, payer contracting)

MARGIN EXPANSION: Path from 25% to 38% EBITDA margin via (a) reducing professional services from 18% to 8% of revenue through self-service onboarding, (b) migrating 40% of customers from on-prem to cloud (2.3x margin uplift), and (c) offshore R&D center (save $12M/yr)
EXIT: Strategic acquirers (Epic, Oracle Health, Veeva) actively consolidating healthcare IT at 10-14x revenue; financial sponsor exits at 8-10x

slides.speaker_notes: The thesis rests on three pillars: regulatory tailwinds making healthcare IT non-discretionary, a dominant market position with extreme switching costs, and a clear operational playbook to expand margins 1,300 basis points. The payer vertical expansion is pure upside -- not modeled in the base case.

CloudMetrics trades at a premium to the median -- 8.3x vs 6.8x revenue -- but the premium is justified by best-in-class growth (32% vs 16% median), NRR (128% vs 115%), and gross margins (82% vs 68%). On a growth-adjusted basis (EV/Revenue/Growth), CloudMetrics is actually cheaper than Veeva and Phreesia.

The DCF yields an implied EV of $2.14B -- 19% above our proposed acquisition price of $1.8B, providing a meaningful margin of safety. The sensitivity table shows that even at the harsh end (12.5% WACC, 10.0x exit), the implied value of $1.59B still represents reasonable downside at only 12% below our entry price.

elements.content: Base case assumes 5-year hold, $600M equity check (33% equity / 67% debt). Bear case assumes growth deceleration to 15% and margin compression. Bull case assumes successful payer vertical launch adding $160M ARR by Year 4.

slides.speaker_notes: The base case delivers a 3.1x MOIC and 28% gross IRR -- well above our 2.5x / 20% Fund VII hurdle. Even the bear case returns 1.5x / 12%, protecting our capital. The home run scenario -- cracking the payer vertical -- is a real possibility given CloudMetrics' existing data assets and two signed LOIs from major payers.

Total leverage of 6.0x LTM EBITDA is within market norms for a high-growth SaaS acquisition. The 82% gross margin and 128% NRR provide strong debt service coverage. We expect to delever to 3.5x by Year 3 through EBITDA growth alone -- no mandatory prepayments required.

rows: Diversification KPI in management incentive plan; no single customer >4%
24-month retention package + equity rollover; CTO succession plan required pre-close
Product pivot to cost reduction (defensive) from revenue optimization (offensive)
Phased migration with rollback; hire VP Cloud Engineering pre-close
Deepen integrations; best-of-breed positioning; M&A for adjacencies
Macro / recession reduces health IT spend
Healthcare IT is 80% non-discretionary regulatory spend; counter-cyclical

slides.speaker_notes: Three risks score 12 -- the highest tier. Customer concentration is real but improving: top 10 was 38% two years ago, now 28%. The CTO retention package and cloud migration VP are must-haves before close -- both are incorporated in our transaction cost estimates.

elements.content: Healthcare IT spending is projected to reach $92B by 2030 (12% CAGR from $48B in 2025). Three secular tailwinds: (1) TEFCA mandate requiring nationwide health data interoperability by 2027, (2) CMS value-based care reimbursement covering 60% of Medicare payments by 2028, (3) AI adoption in clinical workflows reaching 45% penetration by 2029. CloudMetrics is positioned at the intersection of all three trends -- its RCM analytics platform is the data foundation required for each regulatory compliance initiative.

items: Epic (dominant in large health systems, weak in mid-market -- CloudMetrics' sweet spot)
Oracle Health / Cerner (enterprise EHR, struggling with integration post-acquisition)
Veeva Systems (life sciences vertical SaaS -- potential acquirer at 15x revenue)
Health Catalyst (direct competitor, 4.1x revenue -- growth decelerating)
Phreesia (patient intake adjacent -- potential M&A target for CloudMetrics)

slides.speaker_notes: The competitive landscape actually works in our favor. Epic dominates large health systems but has minimal mid-market presence. Oracle is distracted by integration issues. Health Catalyst is the closest comp but growing at half CloudMetrics' rate. The real strategic question is whether we can position CloudMetrics as the acquisition target for Epic or Veeva at a premium exit.

elements.content:
Requesting approval for a $600M equity commitment from Fund VII to acquire CloudMetrics at $1.8B enterprise value (8.3x NTM revenue). Base case: 3.1x MOIC / 28% IRR over 5 years. Exclusivity expires April 15, 2026.

Next Steps: (1) Approve equity commitment, (2) Engage Quality of Earnings firm, (3) Finalize debt commitment letters, (4) Execute management presentations.

Apex Capital Partners | Fund VII | Strictly Confidential

slides.speaker_notes: We need IC approval today to maintain our exclusivity window. The founder has indicated willingness to roll 10% equity and stay as CTO for 3 years. Our conviction level is high -- this is the best-positioned asset we have seen in healthcare IT in Fund VII's investment period.
